title: phanera kockiana var. brevipedicellata
intro: Phanera kockiana var. brevipedicellata, also known as a type of climbing plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae, is a species of plant native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Phanera kockiana var. brevipedicellata are said to include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Phanera kockiana var. brevipedicellata often involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's roots, leaves, or stems, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
